rules.description: Space-surrounded em dashes, against typographic convention. The guide is explicit this is weak alone and most useful in combination; the tier-2 clustering gate enforces that.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Overuse of em dashes

positive: The chain — a modular design — rotates C2 — constantly.
negative: The chain—a modular design—rotates C2 constantly.

rules.description: Curly quotation marks and apostrophes. Guide caveat applies in full: Word smart quotes, macOS defaults, and Chicago style all produce these, so this must never fire alone and carries near-zero weight. Presence narrows the vendor estimate away from Gemini and Claude, which typically emit straight quotes.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Curly quotation marks and apostrophes

positive: The group’s tooling “rotates” infrastructure, and it’s the operator’s habit.
negative: The group's tooling "rotates" infrastructure, and it's the operator's habit.

rules.description: Emoji used as document decoration. Analytic writing has no emoji; section-marker emoji are an LLM formatting default.
Inline-header vertical lists: every bullet shaped as '- **Header**: text'.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Inline-header vertical lists

negative:
- phishing for initial access
- a scheduled task for persistence
- HTTPS exfil

rules.description: Thematic break (---) immediately before a heading, an LLM sectioning habit. Weak alone; gated by tier-2 clustering.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Horizontal rules
